import io
import string
import list

fn is_ws(_ b: Int) -> Bool
  b == 32 or b == 9 or b == 10 or b == 13
end

fn main() -> Unit
  let input = io.read_to_end()
  let n = string.len(input)
  mut nums: List<Int> = []
  mut i = 0
  while i < n
    mut skipping = true
    while skipping and i < n
      match string.byte_at(input, i)
      when Some(b)
        if is_ws(b)
          i = i + 1
        else
          skipping = false
        end
      when None
        skipping = false
      end
    end
    if i < n
      mut sign = 1
      match string.byte_at(input, i)
      when Some(b)
        if b == 45
          sign = -1
          i = i + 1
        else if b == 43
          i = i + 1
        end
      when None
        ()
      end
      mut value = 0
      mut parsing = true
      while parsing and i < n
        match string.byte_at(input, i)
        when Some(b)
          if b >= 48 and b <= 57
            value = value * 10 + (b - 48)
            i = i + 1
          else
            parsing = false
          end
        when None
          parsing = false
        end
      end
      nums = list.push(nums, value * sign)
    end
  end

  mut unique: List<Int> = []
  for x in nums
    if not list.contains(unique, x)
      unique = list.push(unique, x)
    end
  end

  mut sorted: List<Int> = []
  for x in unique
    mut new_sorted: List<Int> = []
    mut inserted = false
    for y in sorted
      if not inserted and x < y
        new_sorted = list.push(new_sorted, x)
        inserted = true
      end
      new_sorted = list.push(new_sorted, y)
    end
    if not inserted
      new_sorted = list.push(new_sorted, x)
    end
    sorted = new_sorted
  end

  mut out = ""
  mut first = true
  for x in sorted
    if first
      out = "#{x}"
      first = false
    else
      out = "#{out} #{x}"
    end
  end
  println(out)
end
